content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule and can identify the neighbor atoms of C:10. Looking at the image, atom C:10 (carbon with index 10) is connected to:

- N:9 (nitrogen with index 9) - to the left
- C:11 (carbon with index 11) - to the right
- C:15 (carbon with index 15) - below/right
- H:24 (hydrogen with index 24) - above/right

Wait, let me double-check the hydrogen atom. In the image, I see H:24 attached to C:10 (not H:28 as I initially thought). The hydrogen atom labeled H:24 is directly bonded to C:10.

Let me confirm the geomconf_index from the file name. The XYZ file is named "geomconf_1944.xyz", so the geomconf_index is 1944.

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of C:10 are:
- Atom index 9, element N
- Atom index 11, element C
- Atom index 15, element C
- Atom index 24, element H

And the geomconf_index for this molecule is 1944.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 10, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
